defs: The repetition of the same letter at the beginning of two or more words immediately succeeding each other, or at short intervals; as in the following lines: -
quotes: Behemoth, biggest born of earth, upheaved His vastness.
Fly o'er waste fens and windy fields.
In a somer seson whan soft was the sonne, I shope me in shroudes as I a shepe were.
notes: ☞ The recurrence of the same letter in accented parts of words is also called alliteration. Anglo-Saxon poetry is characterized by alliterative meter of this sort. Later poets also employed it.
